AUTO-GENERATED by PyAutoBuild — do not edit by hand; regenerate with generate.py.

# PyAutoGalaxy Workspace — Full Catalogue

> Complete, generated listing of every script (and its matching notebook) in this
> workspace, grouped by top-level `scripts/` folder. This is the expanded companion to
> the curated `llms.txt` routing layer. Each entry links the script's title to its path
> and gives the first line of its docstring; `Contents:` lists the sections within.

## imaging

- [Imaging: Data Preparation](scripts/imaging/data_preparation.py): When a CCD imaging dataset is analysed, it must conform to certain standards in order for the analysis to be performed correctly. This tutorial describes these standards and links to more detailed scripts which will help you prepare your dataset to adhere to them if it does not already.
  - Contents: Pixel Scale, Image, Noise Map, PSF, Data Processing Complete, Mask (Optional), Light Centre (Optional), Extra Galaxies (Optional), Mask Extra Galaxies (Optional), Info (Optional)
- [Data Preparation: Image](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/examples/data.py): The image is the image of your galaxy, which comes from a telescope like the Hubble Space telescope (HST).
  - Contents: Loading Data From Individual Fits Files, Converting Data To Electrons Per Second, Resizing Data, Background Subtraction
- [Data Preparation: Noise-map](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/examples/noise_map.py): The noise-map defines the uncertainty in every pixel of your galaxy image, where values are defined as the RMS standard deviation in every pixel (not the variances, HST WHT-map values, etc.).
  - Contents: Loading Data From Individual Fits Files, 1) Tools Illustrated In Image, Noise Conversions
- [Data Preparation: Extra Galaxies (Optional)](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/examples/optional/extra_galaxies_centres.py): There may be extra galaxies nearby the main galaxy, whose emission blends with the galaxy.
  - Contents: Masking Extra Galaxies, Links / Resources, Output
- [Data Preparation: Info (Optional)](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/examples/optional/info.py): Auxiliary information about a galaxy dataset may used during an analysis or afterwards when interpreting the modeling results. For example, the redshifts of the source and galaxy.
- [Data Preparation: Lens Light Centre (Optional)](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/examples/optional/light_centre.py): This script marks the (y,x) arcsecond locations of the galaxy light centre(s) of the galaxy you are analysing. These can be used as fixed values for the galaxy light and mass models in a model-fit.
- [Data Preparation: Mask (Optional)](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/examples/optional/mask.py): The mask removes the regions of the image where the galaxy  galaxy are not present, typically the edges of the image.
- [Data Preparation: Extra Galaxies Mask (Optional)](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/examples/optional/mask_extra_galaxies.py): There may be regions of an image that have signal near the galaxy that is from other galaxies not associated with the main galaxy we are studying. The emission from these images will impact our model fitting and needs to be removed from the analysis.
  - Contents: Links / Resources, Output
- [Data Preparation: PSF](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/examples/psf.py): The Point Spread Function (PSF) describes blurring due the optics of your dataset`s telescope. It is used by PyAutoGalaxy when fitting a dataset to include these effects, such that does not bias the model.
  - Contents: Loading Data From Individual Fits Files, 1) PSF Size, PSF Dimensions, PSF Normalization
- [GUI Preprocessing: Extra Galaxies](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/gui/extra_galaxies_centres.py): There may be extra galaxies nearby the main galaxy, whose emission blends with the galaxy.
  - Contents: Dataset, Search Box, Clicker, Output
- [GUI Preprocessing: Lens Light Centre](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/gui/light_centre.py): This tool allows one to input the galaxy light centre(s) of a galaxy(es) via a GUI, which can be used as a fixed value in pipelines.
  - Contents: Dataset, Search Box, Clicker, Output
- [GUI Preprocessing: Mask](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/gui/mask.py): This tool allows one to mask a bespoke mask for a given image of a galaxy using an interactive GUI. This mask can then be loaded before a pipeline is run and passed to that pipeline so as to become the default masked used by a search (if a mask function is not passed to that search).
  - Contents: Dataset, Scribbler, Output
- [GUI Preprocessing: Extra Galaxies Mask (Optional)](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/gui/mask_extra_galaxies.py): There may be regions of an image that have signal near the galaxy that is from other galaxies not associated with the main galaxy we are studying. The emission from these images will impact our model fitting and needs to be removed from the analysis.
  - Contents: Dataset, Mask, Scribbler, Output
- [Manual Preprocessing: Mask Irregular](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/manual/mask_irregular.py): (no summary in script docstring)
- [Imaging: Data Preparation](scripts/imaging/data_preparation/start_here.py): When a CCD imaging dataset is analysed, it must conform to certain standards in order for the analysis to be performed correctly. This tutorial describes these standards and links to more detailed scripts which will help you prepare your dataset to adhere to them if it does not already.
  - Contents: Image, Noise Map, PSF, Data Processing Complete, Mask (Optional), Light Centre (Optional), Extra Galaxies (Optional), Mask Extra Galaxies (Optional), Info (Optional)
- [Modeling Features: Extra Galaxies](scripts/imaging/features/extra_galaxies/modeling.py): There may be extra galaxies nearby the main galaxy, whose emission blends with the main galaxy.
  - Contents: Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Extra Galaxies Over Sampling, Extra Galaxies Noise Scaling, Extra Galaxies Dataset, Extra Galaxies Centres, Model, Extra Galaxies Model: Two Options, Search + Analysis, VRAM, Run Time, Model-Fit, Result, Approaches to Extra Galaxies, Multi Gaussian Expansion, Scaling Relations, Wrap Up
- [Simulator: Extra Galaxies](scripts/imaging/features/extra_galaxies/simulator.py): Certain galaxies have extra galaxies nearby their emission, which may overlap the emission of the galaxy we are interested in.
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Galaxies, Extra Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Mask Extra Galaxies, Plane Output
- [Modeling Features: Linear Light Profiles Fit](scripts/imaging/features/linear_light_profiles/fit.py): A "linear light profile" is a variant of a standard light profile where the `intensity` parameter is solved for via linear algebra every time the model is fitted to the data. This uses a process called an "inversion" and it always computes the `intensity` values that give the best fit to the data (e.g. maximize the likelihood) given the light profile's other parameters.
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, Positive Only Solver, Dataset & Mask, Fit, Intensities, Visualization, Linear Objects (Source Code)
- [__Log Likelihood Function: Linear Light Profile__](scripts/imaging/features/linear_light_profiles/likelihood_function.py): This script provides a step-by-step guide of the `log_likelihood_function` which is used to fit `Imaging` data with parametric linear light profiles (e.g. a Sersic bulge and Exponential disk).
  - Contents: Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Masked Image Grid, Linear Light Profiles, LightProfileLinearObjFuncList, Mapping Matrix, Blurred Mapping Matrix ($f$), Data Vector (D), Curvature Matrix (F), Reconstruction (Positive-Negative), Reconstruction (Positive Only), Image Reconstruction, Likelihood Function, Chi Squared, Noise Normalization Term, Calculate The Log Likelihood, Fit, Galaxy Modeling, Wrap Up
- [Modeling Features: Linear Light Profiles](scripts/imaging/features/linear_light_profiles/modeling.py): A "linear light profile" is a variant of a standard light profile where the `intensity` parameter is solved for via linear algebra every time the model is fitted to the data. This uses a process called an "inversion" and it always computes the `intensity` values that give the best fit to the data (e.g. maximize the likelihood) given the light profile's other parameters.
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, Positive Only Solver, Dataset & Mask, Fit, Intensities, Model, Search & Analysis, Run Time, Model-Fit, Result & Intensities, Visualization, Linear Objects (Source Code)
- [Modeling Features: Multi Gaussian Expansion](scripts/imaging/features/multi_gaussian_expansion/fit.py): A multi Gaussian expansion (MGE) decomposes the galaxy light into ~15-100 Gaussians, where the `intensity` of every Gaussian is solved for via a linear algebra using a process called an "inversion" (see the `linear_light_profiles.py` feature for a full description of this).
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, Positive Only Solver, MGE Source Galaxy, Dataset & Mask, Basis, Gaussians, Linear Light Profiles, Fit, Intensities
- [__Log Likelihood Function: Multi Gaussian Expansion__](scripts/imaging/features/multi_gaussian_expansion/likelihood_function.py): This script provides a step-by-step guide of the `log_likelihood_function` which is used to fit `Imaging` data with a multi-Gaussian expansion (MGE), which is a superposition of multiple 2D Gaussian linear light profiles.
  - Contents: Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Masked Image Grid, Multiple Gaussians & Linear Light Profiles, Basis, Comparison To Linear Light Profiles Example, LightProfileLinearObjFuncList, Mapping Matrix, Blurred Mapping Matrix ($f$), Data Vector (D), Curvature Matrix (F), Reconstruction (Positive-Negative), Reconstruction (Positive Only), Image Reconstruction, Likelihood Function, Chi Squared, Noise Normalization Term, Calculate The Log Likelihood, Fit, Galaxy Modeling, Wrap Up
- [Modeling Features: Multi Gaussian Expansion](scripts/imaging/features/multi_gaussian_expansion/modeling.py): A multi Gaussian expansion (MGE) decomposes the galaxy light into ~15-100 Gaussians, where the `intensity` of every Gaussian is solved for via a linear algebra using a process called an "inversion" (see the `linear_light_profiles.py` feature for a full description of this).
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, Positive Only Solver, MGE Source Galaxy, Dataset & Mask, Model, Search & Analysis, Run Time, Model-Fit, Result, MGE Source, Regularization
- [Simulator: Light MGE](scripts/imaging/features/multi_gaussian_expansion/simulator.py): This script simulates `Imaging` of a galaxy using light profiles where:
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Plane Output
- [Modeling: Light Parametric Operated](scripts/imaging/features/operated_light_profile/modeling.py): (no summary in script docstring)
  - Contents: Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Over Sampling, Model, Search, Analysis, VRAM, Run Time, Model-Fit, Result
- [Simulator: Sersic + Exp](scripts/imaging/features/operated_light_profile/simulator.py): This script simulates `Imaging` of a galaxy using light profiles where:
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Plane Output
- [Features: Pixelization Fit](scripts/imaging/features/pixelization/fit.py): This script performs a single, direct fit (no non-linear search) of a galaxy that has two components:
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, Positive Only Solver, Dataset & Mask, Mesh Shape, Pixelization, Fit, Mask Extra Galaxies, Linear Objects / Grids / Reconstruction
- [Pixelization: Galaxy Reconstruction](scripts/imaging/features/pixelization/galaxy_reconstruction.py): A common pixelization use-case is to reconstruct the irregular component of a galaxy's surface brightness on a pixelization mesh, and then export that reconstruction to perform scientific analysis.
  - Contents: Model Fit, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Reconstruction CSV
- [__Log Likelihood Function: Pixelization__](scripts/imaging/features/pixelization/likelihood_function.py): This script provides a step-by-step guide of the **PyAutoGalaxy** `log_likelihood_function` which is used to fit `Imaging` data with a pixelization (specifically a `RectangularAdaptDensity` mesh and `Constant` regularization scheme).
  - Contents: Mesh Shape, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Over Sampling, Masked Image Grid, Galaxy, Galaxy Pixelization and Regularization, Interpolation, Mapper, Over Sampling, Alternative Meshes, Mapping Matrix, Blurred Mapping Matrix ($f$), Data Vector (D), Curvature Matrix (F), Regularization Matrix (H), F + Lamdba H, Galaxy Reconstruction (s), Image Reconstruction, Likelihood Function, Chi Squared, Regularization Term, Complexity Terms, Noise Normalization Term, Calculate The Log Likelihood, Fit, Galaxy Modeling, Log Likelihood Function: Pixelization With Light Profile, Wrap Up
- [Features: Pixelization Modeling](scripts/imaging/features/pixelization/modeling.py): This is the canonical example of when (and why) you should reach for a pixelization in **PyAutoGalaxy**.
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, Positive Only Solver, Dataset & Mask, Pixelization, Model, Search & Analysis, Run Time, Model-Fit, Result, Chaining, Result (Advanced), Wrap Up
- [Simulator: Clumpy Galaxy](scripts/imaging/features/pixelization/simulator.py): This script simulates `Imaging` of a galaxy whose light has two distinct components:
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Over Sampling, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Mask Extra Galaxies, Plane Output
- [Modeling Features: Shapelets](scripts/imaging/features/shapelets/fit.py): A shapelet is a basis function that is appropriate for capturing the exponential / disk-like features of a galaxy. It has been employed in galaxy structure studies to model the light of the galaxy, because it can represent features of disky star forming galaxies that a single Sersic function cannot.
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, Dataset & Mask, Basis, Coefficients, Linear Light Profiles, Fit, Intensities, Model, Search & Analysis, Run Time, Model-Fit, Result, Cartesian Shapelets
- [Modeling Features: Shapelets](scripts/imaging/features/shapelets/modeling.py): A shapelet is a basis function that is appropriate for capturing the exponential / disk-like features of a galaxy. It has been employed in galaxy structure studies to model the light of the galaxy, because it can represent features of disky star forming galaxies that a single Sersic function cannot.
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, Dataset & Mask, Basis, Coefficients, Linear Light Profiles, Fit, Intensities, Model, Search & Analysis, Run Time, Model-Fit, Result, Cartesian Shapelets, Lens Shapelets, Regularization
- [Simulator: Manual Signal to Noise Ratio](scripts/imaging/features/simulator_manual_signal_to_noise.py): When simulating `Imaging` of a galaxy, one is often not concerned with the actual units of the light (e.g. electrons per second, counts, etc.) but instead simple wants the data to correspond to a certain signal to noise value.
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Plane Output
- [Modeling Features: Sky Background](scripts/imaging/features/sky_background/fit.py): The background of an image is the light that is not associated with the galaxy we are interested in. This is due to light from the sky, zodiacal light, and light from other galaxies in the field of view.
  - Contents: Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Over Sampling, Fit, Wrap Up
- [Modeling Features: Sky Background](scripts/imaging/features/sky_background/modeling.py): The background of an image is the light that is not associated with the galaxy we are interested in. This is due to light from the sky, zodiacal light, and light from other galaxies in the field of view.
  - Contents: Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Over Sampling, Model, Search, Analysis, VRAM, Run Time, Model-Fit, Result, Wrap Up
- [Simulator: Sky Background](scripts/imaging/features/sky_background/simulator.py): This script simulates `Imaging` of a galaxy where the sky background is not subtracted from the image and therefore appears in the dataset.
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Plane Output
- [Fits](scripts/imaging/fit.py): This guide shows how to fit data using the `FitImaging` object, including visualizing and interpreting its results.
  - Contents: Loading Data, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Grid, Mask, Fitting, Bad Fit, Fit Quantities, Figures of Merit, Galaxy Quantities, Unmasked Quantities, Mask, Pixel Counting, Outputting Results, Modeling Results, Wrap Up
- [__Log Likelihood Function: Light Profile__](scripts/imaging/likelihood_function.py): This script provides a step-by-step guide of the `log_likelihood_function` which is used to fit `Imaging` data with parametric light profiles (e.g. a Sersic bulge and Exponential disk).
  - Contents: Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Over Sampling, Masked Image Grid, Light Profiles (Setup), Galaxy, Galaxy Image, Convolution, Likelihood Function, Chi Squared, Noise Normalization Term, Calculate The Log Likelihood, Fit, Galaxy Modeling, Wrap Up
- [Modeling: Start Here](scripts/imaging/modeling.py): This script is the starting point for modeling of CCD imaging data (E.g. Hubble Space Telescope, Euclid) with **PyAutoGalaxy** and it provides an overview of the modeling API.
  - Contents: Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Over Sampling, Model, Search, Live Visual Update, Analysis, VRAM Use, Run Times, Model-Fit, Output Folder Layout, Result, Features, Data Preparation, HowToGalaxy
- [Simulator: Sersic + Exp](scripts/imaging/simulator.py): This script simulates `Imaging` of a galaxy using light profiles where:
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Over Sampling, PSF Convolution, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Plane Output
- [Simulator: Sample Power-Law](scripts/imaging/simulator_sample.py): This script simulates a sample of `Imaging` datasets of galaxies where:
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Sample Model Distributions, Sample Instances, Output, Visualize, Plane Output
- [Simulator: Sersic](scripts/imaging/simulator_sersic.py): This script simulates `Imaging` of a galaxy using light profiles where:
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Mask Extra Galaxies, Plane Output
- [Start Here: Imaging](scripts/imaging/start_here.py): Galaxies are often observed with CCD imaging, for example using HST, JWST, or ground-based telescopes.
  - Contents: Google Colab Setup, Imports, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Extra Galaxy Removal, Masking, Model, Model Fit, Live Visual Update, Result, Extra Galaxy Removal GUI, Model Your Own Galaxy, Simulator, Sample, Wrap Up

## interferometer

- [THIS SCRIPT IS INCOMPLETE AND IN DEVELOPMENT BUT IT HOPEFULLY IS INFORMATIVE ENOUGH TO HELP YOU GET STARTED.](scripts/interferometer/casa_reduction.py): If not contact us on SLACK!
- [Interferometer: Data Preparation](scripts/interferometer/data_preparation.py): When an interferometer dataset is analysed, it must conform to certain standards in order for the analysis to be performed correctly. This tutorial describes these standards and links to more detailed scripts which will help you prepare your dataset to adhere to them if it does not already.
  - Contents: SLACK, Pixel Scale, Visibilities, Noise-Map, UV Wavelengths, Real Space Mask, Data Processing Complete, Light Centre (Optional), Extra Galaxies (Optional), Mask Extra Galaxies (Optional), Info (Optional)
- [Modeling Features: Extra Galaxies (Interferometer)](scripts/interferometer/features/extra_galaxies/modeling.py): There may be extra galaxies nearby the main galaxy whose light blends with the main galaxy's emission. For interferometer data — unlike strong-lensing imaging — we typically *want* to detect these extra galaxies and fit their light, because autogalaxy is a morphology-fitting tool for multi-galaxy fields rather than a gravitational-lensing tool.
  - Contents: Data Preparation, Mask, Dataset, Extra Galaxies Centres, Model, Extra Galaxies Model, Search + Analysis, VRAM, Run Time, Result, Approaches to Extra Galaxies, Wrap Up
- [Simulator: Extra Galaxies (Interferometer)](scripts/interferometer/features/extra_galaxies/simulator.py): Certain galaxies have extra galaxies nearby their emission, which may overlap the emission of the galaxy we are interested in. For interferometer data — unlike strong-lensing imaging — we typically *want* to detect the extra galaxies and fit their light, because autogalaxy is a morphology-fitting tool for multi-galaxy fields rather than a gravitational-lensing tool.
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, uv-wavelengths, Simulator, Galaxies, Output, Galaxies json, Extra Galaxies Centres
- [Modeling Features: Linear Light Profiles Fit (Interferometer)](scripts/interferometer/features/linear_light_profiles/fit.py): A "linear light profile" is a variant of a standard light profile where the `intensity` parameter is solved for via linear algebra every time the model is fitted to the data. This uses a process called an "inversion" and it always computes the `intensity` values that give the best fit to the data (e.g. maximize the likelihood) given the light profile's other parameters.
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, Positive Only Solver, Model, Mask, Dataset, Fit, Intensities, Visualization, Wrap Up
- [__Log Likelihood Function: Linear Light Profile (Interferometer)__](scripts/interferometer/features/linear_light_profiles/likelihood_function.py): This script provides a step-by-step guide of the `log_likelihood_function` which is used to fit `Interferometer` data with parametric linear light profiles (e.g. a linear `Sersic` bulge and linear `Exponential` disk).
  - Contents: Prerequisites, Mask, Dataset, Galaxy Linear Light Profiles, Internal Intensity, Mapping Matrix, Transformed Mapping Matrix ($f$), Data Vector (D), Curvature Matrix (F), Reconstruction (Positive-Negative), Reconstruction (Positive Only), Visibilities Reconstruction, Likelihood Function, Chi Squared, Noise Normalization Term, Calculate The Log Likelihood, Fit, Galaxy Modeling, Wrap Up
- [Modeling Features: Linear Light Profiles (Interferometer)](scripts/interferometer/features/linear_light_profiles/modeling.py): A "linear light profile" is a variant of a standard light profile where the `intensity` parameter is solved for via linear algebra every time the model is fitted to the data. This uses a process called an "inversion" and it always computes the `intensity` values that give the best fit to the data (e.g. maximize the likelihood) given the light profile's other parameters.
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, NUFFT (nufftax), Positive Only Solver, Model, Mask, Dataset, Over Sampling, Search, Analysis, VRAM, Run Time, Result, Intensities, Visualization, Max Likelihood Inversion, Linear Objects (Internal Source Code), Wrap Up
- [Modeling Features: Multi Gaussian Expansion Fit (Interferometer)](scripts/interferometer/features/multi_gaussian_expansion/fit.py): A multi-Gaussian expansion (MGE) decomposes a galaxy's light into ~15-100 Gaussians, where the `intensity` of every Gaussian is solved for via linear algebra using a process called an "inversion".
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, Positive Only Solver, Model, Mask, Dataset, Basis, Fit, Intensities, Visualization, Wrap Up
- [__Log Likelihood Function: Multi Gaussian Expansion (Interferometer)__](scripts/interferometer/features/multi_gaussian_expansion/likelihood_function.py): This script provides a step-by-step guide of the `log_likelihood_function` which is used to fit `Interferometer` data with a multi-Gaussian expansion (MGE): a `Basis` of many linear `Gaussian` profiles whose `intensity` values are solved for analytically via a linear inversion.
  - Contents: Prerequisites, Mask, Dataset, Galaxy MGE Basis, Mapping Matrix, Transformed Mapping Matrix ($f$), Data Vector (D), Curvature Matrix (F), Reconstruction (Positive-Negative), Reconstruction (Positive Only), Visibilities Reconstruction, Likelihood Function, Chi Squared, Noise Normalization Term, Calculate The Log Likelihood, Fit, Galaxy Modeling, Wrap Up
- [Modeling Features: Multi Gaussian Expansion (Interferometer)](scripts/interferometer/features/multi_gaussian_expansion/modeling.py): A multi-Gaussian expansion (MGE) decomposes a galaxy's light into ~15-100 Gaussians, where the `intensity` of every Gaussian is solved for via linear algebra using a process called an "inversion" (see the `linear_light_profiles` feature for a full description of this).
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, NUFFT (nufftax), Positive Only Solver, Model, Mask, Dataset, Over Sampling, Search, Analysis, VRAM, Run Time, Result, Wrap Up
- [Features: Pixelization Fit](scripts/interferometer/features/pixelization/fit.py): This script performs a single, direct fit (no non-linear search) of the `clumpy` interferometer dataset — a galaxy with a smooth central bulge plus asymmetric clumpy star formation. The fit uses a pixelization with a rectangular mesh and constant regularization scheme to reconstruct the galaxy's surface brightness on a pixel grid.
  - Contents: Advantages, Disadvantages, Positive Only Solver, Mask, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Sparse Operators, Settings, Over Sampling, Mesh Shape, Pixelization, Fit, Wrap Up, Linear Objects, Grids, Reconstruction, Mapped Reconstructed Images, Linear Algebra Matrices (Advanced), Evidence Terms (Advanced), Simulated Interferometer, Future Ideas / Contributions
- [Pixelization: Galaxy Reconstruction](scripts/interferometer/features/pixelization/galaxy_reconstruction.py): A common pixelization use-case is to reconstruct a galaxy’s surface brightness on a pixelization mesh, and then export this reconstruction to perform scientific analysis.
  - Contents: Model Fit, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Reconstruction CSV
- [__Log Likelihood Function: Pixelization__](scripts/interferometer/features/pixelization/likelihood_function.py): This script provides a step-by-step guide of the **PyAutoGalaxy** `log_likelihood_function` which is used to fit `Interferometer` data with an inversion (specifically a `RectangularUniform` mesh and `Constant` regularization scheme`).
  - Contents: Mesh Shape, Mask, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Over Sampling, Masked Image Grid, Galaxy, Rectangular Mesh, Interpolation, Mapper, Over Sampling, Alternative Meshes, Mapping Matrix, Transformed Mapping Matrix ($f$), Data Vector (D), Curvature Matrix (F), Regularization Matrix (H), F + Lamdba H, Galaxy Reconstruction (s), Visibilities Reconstruction, Likelihood Function, Chi Squared, Regularization Term, Complexity Terms, Noise Normalization Term, Calculate The Log Likelihood, Fit, Galaxy Modeling, Log Likelihood Function: Pixelization With Light Profile, Log Likelihood Function: Fast Chi Squared, Wrap Up
- [Pixelization: Many Visibilities Preparation](scripts/interferometer/features/pixelization/many_visibilities_preparation.py): To perform many visibility modeling, a matrix called `nufft_precision_operator` is created and used, which encodes information and symmetries into the Fourier transform operation performed when modeling interferometer datasets, in a way that exploits the sparsity of the pixelized galaxy reconstructions and means a very small amount of memory or VRAM is used.
  - Contents: High Resolution Dataset, Dataset + Masking, Profiling Dataset, Curvature Preload, Curvature Preload Output, Wrap Up
- [Features: Pixelization](scripts/interferometer/features/pixelization/modeling.py): This is the canonical example of when (and why) you should reach for a pixelization to model interferometer visibilities of a galaxy in **PyAutoGalaxy**.
  - Contents: Advantages, Disadvantages, Positive Only Solver, Model, Mask, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Sparse Operators, Settings, Over Sampling, Mesh Shape, Model, Search, Analysis, VRAM, Run Time, Model-Fit, Result, Wrap Up, Chaining, HowToGalaxy, Future Ideas / Contributions
- [Simulator: Clumpy Galaxy (Interferometer)](scripts/interferometer/features/pixelization/simulator.py): This script simulates `Interferometer` data of a galaxy whose real-space light has two distinct components:
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, UV Wavelengths, Simulator, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Plane Output
- [Modeling Features: Shapelets Fit (Interferometer)](scripts/interferometer/features/shapelets/fit.py): A shapelet is a basis function appropriate for capturing the exponential / disk-like features of a galaxy. The `intensity` of every shapelet is solved for via linear algebra ("inversion") rather than being a free parameter of the non-linear search.
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, Positive Negative Solver, Model, Mask, Dataset, Basis, Fit, Intensities, Visualization, Wrap Up
- [Modeling Features: Shapelets (Interferometer)](scripts/interferometer/features/shapelets/modeling.py): A shapelet is a basis function appropriate for capturing the exponential / disk-like features of a galaxy. It has been employed in galaxy-structure studies to model galaxy light because it can represent disky star-forming features that a single Sersic function cannot.
  - Contents: Advantages & Disadvantages, NUFFT (nufftax), Positive Negative Solver, Model, Mask, Dataset, Over Sampling, Search, Analysis, VRAM, Run Time, Result, Wrap Up
- [Fits](scripts/interferometer/fit.py): This guide shows how to fit data using the `FitInterferometer` object, including visualizing and interpreting its results.
  - Contents: Mask, Loading Data, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Fitting, Bad Fit, Fit Quantities, Figures of Merit, Galaxy Quantities, Outputting Results
- [__Log Likelihood Function: Parametric__](scripts/interferometer/likelihood_function.py): This script provides a step-by-step guide of the `log_likelihood_function` which is used to fit `Interferometer` data with parametric light profiles (e.g. a Sersic bulge and Exponential disk).
  - Contents: Mask, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Over Sampling, Masked Image Grid, Light Profiles (Setup), Galaxy, Galaxy Image, Fourier Transform, Likelihood Function, Chi Squared, Noise Normalization Term, Calculate The Log Likelihood, Fit, Galaxy Modeling, Wrap Up
- [Modeling: Start Here](scripts/interferometer/modeling.py): This script is the starting point for modeling of interferometer datasets (e.g. SMA, ALMA) and it provides an overview of the modeling API. The same workflow scales from a few hundred visibilities to many millions, thanks to the JAX-native `TransformerNUFFT` (backed by `nufftax`).
  - Contents: Number of Visibilities, Model, Mask, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Over Sampling, Model, Search, Live Visual Update, Analysis, VRAM Use, Run Times, Model-Fit, Output Folder Layout, Result, Features, Data Preparation, HowToGalaxy, Modeling Customization
- [Simulator: Sersic](scripts/interferometer/simulator.py): This script simulates `Interferometer` data of a galaxy where:
  - Contents: Grid, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Plane Output
- [Start Here: Interferometer](scripts/interferometer/start_here.py): Galaxies are observed with radio/mm interferometers (e.g. ALMA), which measure complex visibilities in the uv-plane instead of CCD images.
  - Contents: JAX, NUFFT (nufftax), Number of Visibilities, Google Colab Setup, Imports, Mask (Real Space), Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Model, Model Fit, Live Visual Update, Result, Model Your Own Galaxy, Simulator, Sample, Wrap Up

## multi

- [Modeling Features: Dataset Offsets](scripts/multi/features/dataset_offsets/modeling.py): Multi-wavelength datasets often have offsets between their images, which are due to the different telescope pointings during the observations.
  - Contents: Advantages, Disadvantages, Model, Colors, Pixel Scales, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Analysis, Model, Search, Model-Fit, Result
- [Simulator: Dataset Offsets](scripts/multi/features/dataset_offsets/simulator.py): Multi-wavelength datasets often have offsets between their images, which are due to the different telescope pointings during the observations.
  - Contents: Colors, Dataset Paths, Simulate, Offset, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Galaxies json
- [Modeling: Imaging & Interferometer](scripts/multi/features/imaging_and_interferometer/modeling.py): This script fits an `Imaging` dataset of a galaxy with a model where:
  - Contents: Benefits, Interferometer Masking, Interferometer Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Imaging Dataset, Imaging Masking, Analysis, Search, Model-Fit, Result
- [Simulator: Mutli Interferometer](scripts/multi/features/imaging_and_interferometer/simulator.py): This script simulates `Interferometer` data of a galaxy where:
  - Contents: Simulate, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Galaxies json
- [Modeling Features: One By One](scripts/multi/features/one_by_one/modeling.py): Multi-wavelength analysis does not necessarily require us to fit all datasets simultaneously. Instead, we can fit one dataset first in order to infer a robust galaxy model, and then fit the next dataset, using the inferred model as the starting point.
  - Contents: Model, Colors, Pixel Scales, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Analysis, Model, Search, Model-Fit, Result, Second Dataset Bulge Model Fixed, Second Dataset Offset
- [Modeling: Pixelized](scripts/multi/features/pixelization/modeling.py): This script fits multiple multi-wavelength `Imaging` datasets of a galaxy with a model where:
  - Contents: Colors, Pixel Scales, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Mesh Shape, Analysis, Model, Search, Model-Fit, Result
- [Modeling: Same Wavelength](scripts/multi/features/same_wavelength/modeling.py): This script fits a multiple `Imaging` datasets observed at the same wavelength of a galaxy with a model where:
  - Contents: Pixel Scales, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Analysis, Model, Search, Model-Fit, Result
- [Simulator: Same Wavelength](scripts/multi/features/same_wavelength/simulator.py): This script simulates multiple `Imaging` datasets of a galaxy where:
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Simulate, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Galaxies json
- [Modeling: Wavelength Dependence](scripts/multi/features/wavelength_dependence/modeling.py): This script fits a multi-wavelength `Imaging` dataset of a galaxy with a model where:
  - Contents: Colors, Wavelengths, Pixel Scales, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Analysis, Model, Model + Analysis, Search, Model-Fit, Result
- [Simulator: Wavelength Dependent](scripts/multi/features/wavelength_dependence/simulator.py): This script simulates multi-wavelength `Imaging` of a galaxy where:
  - Contents: Colors, Wavelengths, Dataset Paths, Simulate, Intensity vs Wavelength, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Galaxies json
- [Modeling: Multi Modeling](scripts/multi/modeling.py): This script fits multiple multi-wavelength `Imaging` datasets of a galaxy with a model where:
  - Contents: Colors, Pixel Scales, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Over Sampling, Model, Analysis List, Analysis Factor, Factor Graph, Search, Live Visual Update, VRAM Use, Model-Fit, Result, Wrap Up
- [Plots: MultiSubPlots](scripts/multi/plot.py): This example illustrates how to produce multiple subplot figures from different data objects, using the function-based plotting API.
  - Contents: Imaging Dataset Subplot, Fit Imaging Subplot, Galaxies Subplot, Output, Wrap Up
- [Simulator: Multi Light Sersic](scripts/multi/simulator.py): This script simulates multi-wavelength `Imaging` of a galaxy where:
  - Contents: Colors, Dataset Paths, Simulate, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Galaxies json
- [Start Here: Multi Wavelength](scripts/multi/start_here.py): Galaxies are often observed with CCD imaging, for example using HST, JWST, or ground-based telescopes.
  - Contents: JAX, Google Colab Setup, Imports, Dataset, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Masking, Model, Analysis, Model Fit, Live Visual Update, Result, Model Your Own Galaxy, Simulator, Wrap Up

## guides

- [Over Sampling](scripts/guides/advanced/over_sampling.py): Throughout the workspace, we have created 2D grids of (y,x) coordinates and input them into light profiles to compute their image.
  - Contents: Illustration, Numerics, Images, Adaptive Over Sampling, Default Over-Sampling, Multiple Galaxies, Dataset & Modeling, Pixelization
- [Data Structures](scripts/guides/data_structures.py): This tutorial illustrates the data structure objects which data and results quantities are stored using, which are extensions of NumPy arrays.
  - Contents: Units, API, Grids, Native, Slim, Masked Data Structures, Data, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Galaxies, Irregular Structures, Vector Quantities
- [Galaxies](scripts/guides/galaxies.py): This tutorial shows how to use galaxies, including visualizing and extracting their individual light profiles.
  - Contents: Units, Data Structures, Grids, Light Profiles, Galaxies, Galaxies (multiple), Log10, Extending Objects, Data Structures Slim / Native, Individual Galaxy Components, Galaxies Composition, One Dimension Projection, One Dimensional Quantities, Decomposed 1D Plot, Modeling Results
- [HPC: Example CPU](scripts/guides/hpc/example_cpu_and_gpu.py): This example illustrates how to set up galaxy modeling on a High Performance Computing (HPC) system using multiple CPUs.
- [Modeling: Parallel Bug Fix](scripts/guides/modeling/bug_fix.py): Depending on the operating system (e.g. Linux, Mac, Windows) and Python version, running a Python script or Jupyter notebook may lead to a error being raised when the search begins.
  - Contents: The Fix, Trouble Shooting
- [Modeling: Chaining](scripts/guides/modeling/chaining.py): Non-linear search chaining is an advanced model-fitting approach which breaks the model-fitting procedure down into multiple non-linear searches, using the results of the initial searches to initialization parameter sampling in subsequent searches. This contrasts the `modeling` examples which each compose and fit a single lens model-fit using one non-linear search.
  - Contents: Dataset + Masking, Paths, Model (Search 1), Search + Analysis + Model-Fit (Search 1), Result (Search 1), Model Chaining, Search + Analysis + Model-Fit (Search 2), Result (Search 2), Detailed Explanation Of Prior Passing, EXAMPLE
- [Model Cookbook](scripts/guides/modeling/cookbook.py): The model cookbook provides a concise reference to model composition tools, specifically the `Model` and `Collection` objects.
  - Contents: Simple Model, More Complex Models, Concise API, Prior Customization, Model Customization, Available Model Components, JSon Outputs, Many Profile Models (Advanced), Model Linking (Advanced), Across Datasets (Advanced), Relations (Advanced), PyAutoFit API, Wrap Up
- [Modeling: Customize](scripts/guides/modeling/customize.py): This script gives a run through of all the different ways the analysis can be customized for modeling, with reasons explaining why each customization is useful.
  - Contents: Dataset, Mask, Over Sampling
- [Modeling: Searches](scripts/guides/modeling/searches.py): This script gives a run through of all non-linear searches that are available for modeling.
  - Contents: Dynesty, Emcee, Zeus, LBFGS, Start Point, Search Cookbook
- [Plots: Pixelization](scripts/guides/plot/advanced/plotters_pixelization.py): This example illustrates the API for plotting pixelized galaxy reconstructions using the new function-based plotting API and dedicated functions like `subplot_of_mapper` for mesh-specific visualizations.
  - Contents: Setup, Fit Imaging, Reconstruction, Inversion Plots, Mapper, Mesh Grids, Delaunay
- [Plots: Mat Plot](scripts/guides/plot/examples/mat_plot.py): This example illustrates the API for customizing the appearance of figures using arguments passed directly to `plot_array` (and related functions like `plot_grid`).
  - Contents: Setup, Output, Figure Size and Aspect, Labels, Color Map, Log10, Contours, Ticks / Colorbar / Legend / Annotate / Text / Axis
- [Plots: Plotters](scripts/guides/plot/examples/plotters.py): This example illustrates the API for plotting using standalone functions, which enable quick visualization of all key quantities.
  - Contents: Setup, Array2D, Grid2D, Light Profile, Galaxy, Galaxies, Imaging, Fit Imaging, Log10, One Dimensional Plots, Output, Probability Density Function (PDF) Plots
- [Plots: Searches](scripts/guides/plot/examples/searches.py): This example illustrates the API for plotting the results of different non-linear searches.
  - Contents: Setup, Dynesty, Emcee, Zeus, GetDist
- [Plots: Visuals](scripts/guides/plot/examples/visuals.py): This example illustrates the API for adding visuals to plots.
  - Contents: Setup, Light Profile Centres, Mask, Origin, Grid, Border, Array Overlay, Patch Overlay, Vector Field
- [Simulator: Sersic x2](scripts/guides/plot/simulator.py): This script simulates `Imaging` of two galaxies where:
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Plane Output
- [Plots: Start Here](scripts/guides/plot/start_here.py): This example illustrates the API for plotting.
  - Contents: Dataset, Figures, Customization, Log10, Configs, Subplots, Visuals, Searches
- [Light Profiles](scripts/guides/profiles/light.py): This guide is the single-page tour of every light profile **PyAutoGalaxy** ships with: how to construct each one, how to evaluate its image on a grid, how to compose it into a model, and how to retrieve an instance from that model.  Once you have read this guide you should be able to recognise every profile referenced by the modelling examples and the API reference, and you should know which family (standard / linear / operated / multipole / basis) any given profile belongs to.
  - Contents: Overview & Docs URL, All Light Profiles (Survey), Detailed Example: Sersic Image, Linear Light Profiles, Operated Light Profiles, Basis, Light Profile in a Model, Model Instance from Light Profile, Multipole Light Profiles, Remaining Profiles Walkthrough
- [Results: Quick Fit Helper](scripts/guides/results/_quick_fit.py): Internal helper invoked via subprocess from the tutorials in this folder. Produces two fast, capped Nautilus fits at ``output/results_folder/`` so the aggregator and workflow examples have a populated results directory to read from.
- [Results: Data Fitting](scripts/guides/results/aggregator/data_fitting.py): In this tutorial, we use the aggregator to load models and data from a non-linear search and use them to perform fits to the data.
  - Contents: Aggregator, Fits via Aggregator, Modification, Visualization Customization, Errors (Random draws from PDF)
- [Results: Galaxies and Fits](scripts/guides/results/aggregator/galaxies_fit.py): This tutorial inspects an inferred model using galaxies inferred by the non-linear search. This allows us to visualize and interpret its results.
  - Contents: Model Fit, Max Likelihood Galaxies, Samples, Refitting
- [This script still needs writing, I have kept some notes on questions asked by users which may help you...](scripts/guides/results/aggregator/interferometer.py): (no summary in script docstring)
- [Results: Models](scripts/guides/results/aggregator/models.py): Suppose we have the results of many fits and we only wanted to load and inspect a specific set of model-fits (e.g. the results of `start_here.py`). We can use querying tools to only load the results we are interested in.
  - Contents: Aggregator, Galaxies via Aggregator, Luminosity Example, Errors (PDF from samples), Errors (Random draws from PDF)
- [Database: Queries](scripts/guides/results/aggregator/queries.py): Suppose we have the results of many fits and we only wanted to load and inspect a specific set of model-fits (e.g. the results of `start_here.py`). We can use querying tools to only load the results we are interested in.
  - Contents: Loading From Hard-disk, Unique Tag, Search Name, Model Queries, Logic
- [Results: Samples](scripts/guides/results/aggregator/samples.py): After a non-linear search has completed, it returns a `Result` object that contains information on samples of the non-linear search, such as the maximum likelihood model instance, the errors on each parameter and the Bayesian evidence.
  - Contents: Model Fit, Info, Plot, Samples, Parameters, Figures of Merit, Instances, Posterior / PDF, Errors, Sample Instance, Search Plots, Maximum Likelihood, Bayesian Evidence, Lists, Latex, Derived Errors (Advanced), Samples Filtering (Advanced)
- [Database: Samples](scripts/guides/results/aggregator/samples_via_aggregator.py): In the script `autogalaxy_workspace/*/results/aggregator/samples.py` we show how to inspect the non-linear search samples from a result.
  - Contents: Files, Generators, Samples, Parameters, Samples Info, Samples Summary, Figures of Merit, Instances, Posterior / PDF, Errors, Sample Instance, Search Plots, Maximum Likelihood, Bayesian Evidence, Lists, Latex, Ordering, Samples Filtering
- [Simulator: Sersic + Exp](scripts/guides/results/database/simulators/light_sersic_exp__0.py): This script simulates `Imaging` of a galaxy using light profiles where:
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Over Sampling, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Plane Output, Info
- [Simulator: Sersic + Exp](scripts/guides/results/database/simulators/light_sersic_exp__1.py): This script simulates `Imaging` of a galaxy using light profiles where:
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Over Sampling, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Plane Output, Info
- [Simulator: Sersic + Exp](scripts/guides/results/database/simulators/light_sersic_exp__2.py): This script simulates `Imaging` of a galaxy using light profiles where:
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Over Sampling, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Plane Output, Info
- [Database: Introduction](scripts/guides/results/database/start_here.py): The default behaviour of model-fitting results output is to be written to hard-disc in folders. These are simple to navigate and manually check.
  - Contents: Unique Identifiers, Dataset, Results From Hard Disk, Building a Database File From an Output Folder, Writing Directly To Database, Files, Generators, Model, Search, Samples, Wrap Up
- [Results: Latent Variables](scripts/guides/results/latent_variables.py): A latent variable is a quantity derived from the model parameters that is not itself sampled by the non-linear search — its posterior is induced by the posterior over the parameters. PyAutoGalaxy ships a curated catalogue of galaxy-level latent variables out of the box, controlled by ``autogalaxy/config/latent.yaml``. This tutorial shows what the catalogue contains, how to toggle individual latents on or off via the workspace config, how to load latent results from a completed fit, and how to extend ``AnalysisImaging`` with a user-defined latent when the curated set isn't enough.
  - Contents: Galaxy Latents in PyAutoGalaxy: The curated default catalogue from ``autogalaxy/config/latent.yaml``., Toggling Latents: The workspace ``config/latent.yaml`` override and why it shadows the library default., Model Fit: Reuse the shared quick fit (``_quick_fit.py``) that produces real latent output., Loading Latent Results: ``analysis.compute_latent_samples`` over a subset of PDF draws, and the two, Extending with a Custom Latent: Subclass ``ag.AnalysisImaging``, override ``LATENT_KEYS`` and, Contributing Upstream: When your custom latent is general enough, consider promoting it to the library.
- [Results: Start Here](scripts/guides/results/start_here.py): After a model-fit completes, nearly everything a user could need is written to the `output/` folder. Most of it can be loaded back into full Python objects with a single line of code, via either `.json` files (for model objects like the `Galaxies`, `Model` and samples) or `.fits` files (for imaging products like the model image, residuals and per-galaxy images).
  - Contents: Model Fit, Info
- [Results: CSV](scripts/guides/results/workflow/csv_make.py): This example is a results workflow example, which means it provides tool to set up an effective workflow inspecting and interpreting the large libraries of modeling results.
  - Contents: Model Fit, Workflow Paths, Aggregator, Adding CSV Columns, Saving the CSV, Customizing CSV Headers, Maximum Likelihood Values, Errors, Column Label List, Latent Variables, Computed Columns
- [Results: Fits Make](scripts/guides/results/workflow/fits_make.py): This example is a results workflow example, which means it provides tool to set up an effective workflow inspecting and interpreting the large libraries of modeling results.
  - Contents: Model Fit, Workflow Paths, Aggregator, Extract Images, Output Single Fits, Output to Folder, CSV Files, Add Extra Fits, Custom Fits Files in Analysis
- [Results: PNG Make](scripts/guides/results/workflow/png_make.py): This example is a results workflow example, which means it provides tool to set up an effective workflow inspecting and interpreting the large libraries of modeling results.
  - Contents: Model Fit, Workflow Paths, Aggregator, Extract Images, Output Single Png, Output to Folder, Combine Images From Subplots, Add Extra Png, Shape Customization, Zoom Png, Custom Subplots in Analysis
- [Units and Cosmology](scripts/guides/units/cosmology.py): This tutorial illustrates how to perform unit conversions from **PyAutoGalaxy**'s internal units (e.g. arc-seconds, electrons per second, dimensionless mass units) to physical units (e.g. kiloparsecs, magnitudes, solar masses).
  - Contents: Errors, Galaxy, Arcsec to Kiloparsec, Brightness Units / Luminosity
- [Flux](scripts/guides/units/flux.py): Absolute flux calibration in Astronomy is the process of converting the number of photons detected by a telescope into a physical unit of luminosity or a magnitude. For example, a luminosity might be given in units of solar luminosities or the brightness of a galaxy quoted as a magnitude in units of AB magnitudes.
  - Contents: Zero Point, Mega Janskys / steradian (MJy/sr): James Webb Space Telescope, Latent Variables

## ellipse

- [Results: Database](scripts/ellipse/database.py): In this tutorial, we use the aggregator to load models and data from a non-linear search and use them to perform ellipse fits to the data.
  - Contents: Aggregator, Ellipses via Aggregator, Fits via Aggregator, Visualization Customization, Errors (Random draws from PDF), Multipoles
- [Fits](scripts/ellipse/fit.py): This guide shows how to fit data using ellipse fitting and the `FitEllipse` object, including visualizing and interpreting its results.
  - Contents: Loading Data, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Ellipse Interpolation, Ellipse Fitting, FitEllipse, Multiple Ellipses, Bad Fit, Fit Quantities, Figures of Merit
- [Modeling](scripts/ellipse/modeling.py): This guide shows how to perform ellipse fitting modeling on data using a non-linear search, including visualizing and interpreting its results.
  - Contents: Loading Data, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Model Composition, Search, Live Visual Update, Analysis, Run Times, Model-Fit, Output Folder Layout, Result, Multiple Ellipses, Final Fit, Masking, Data Preparation, HowToGalaxy
- [Multipoles](scripts/ellipse/multipoles.py): This guide shows how to perform ellipse fitting and modeling with multipoles modeling.
  - Contents: Loading Data, Dataset Auto-Simulation, Mask, Multipole Fit, Multipole Order, Multiple Perturbed Ellipses, Modeling, Search, Analysis, Run Times, Model-Fit, Result, Multiple Ellipses, Final Fit, Masking
- [Simulator: Ellipse](scripts/ellipse/simulator.py): Ellipse fitting is a common technique in astronomy to determine the shape of a galaxy, which fits a super position of multiple elliptical isophotes to an image of a galaxy. This allows the ellipticity and position angle of a galaxy to be determined.
  - Contents: Dataset Paths, Grid, Galaxies, Output, Visualize, Plane Output
